key_insight: LLMSurvival turns censored survival prediction into a pairwise ranking problem, enabling unmodified LLMs to learn end-to-end risk models from tabular clinical data.
engineering_relevance: The approach offers a portable, local-deployable alternative to traditional survival models and expert scores for failure/risk prediction in real-world engineering and medical decision settings.
method: Reformulate time-to-event prediction as comparisons between comparable subjects.
Fine-tune standard LLMs on tabular data using censoring-aware pairwise ranking.
Estimate test-time risk by aggregating comparisons against anchor patients from training.
evidence_signal: Evaluated on ICU mortality (MIMIC-IV) and fragility fracture prediction (NewYork-Presbyterian/Weill Cornell), with improved concordance over Cox models and established deep survival baselines.
watch_item: Performance depends on the quality and coverage of anchor comparisons, so cohort shift or sparse comparable pairs could reduce reliability.
paper_title: Towards end-to-end LLM-based censoring-aware survival analysis
